mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 325e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 325e (M20B27) engine typically costs £1,500–£2,800 supply only. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on location and workshop rates. Used engines start from £300–£800 supply only. A full rebuild with timing belt, water pump and gaskets costs around £1,500–£2,800.

Yes — the M20B27 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most durable engines. Many owners report 200,000+ miles with proper maintenance. The engine was designed for efficiency and low-rev torque, with a robust cast iron block and simple SOHC design. However, the timing belt must be replaced every 60,000 miles or 4 years — failure is catastrophic.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 325e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issues are: (1) Timing belt failure if not replaced on schedule — this is catastrophic and the most common cause of engine death, (2) Vacuum leaks causing idle issues — rubber components age and crack, (3) Ageing cooling system components — hoses, radiator, water pump and thermostat, and (4) Rust — the real enemy of E30s, not the engine.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 325e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £4,000–£10,000 325e (E30 values have risen significantly), a £2,200 recon is 20–55% of the car's value — worth it for a clean example. For a car with significant rust, evaluate restoration costs before committing to an engine replacement.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 325e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 325e uses the M20B27 engine. Early cars (1984–1987) are the standard "Eta" with 121 hp and a 4,800 rpm redline. The 1988 model year is the "Super Eta" with 127 hp, a 5,500 rpm redline, and 325i cylinder head components. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: How often should the timing belt be replaced on the 325e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing belt must be replaced every 60,000 miles or 4 years — whichever comes first. The M20B27 is an interference engine — if the belt breaks, pistons will hit valves. This results in bent valves at minimum, all the way to catastrophic damage requiring a full engine replacement. If you don't know when the belt was last changed, assume it needs doing immediately.

eraMap.title: BMW 325e — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.7L SOHC six — low-rev torque monster, 121 hp. Timing belt is the single critical maintenance item
Final-year "Super Eta" — 127 hp, 5500 rpm redline, 325i head & Motronic 1.1

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 325e engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). A timing belt and water pump replacement — the single most critical maintenance item — typically costs £300–£600 parts and labour.

cta.label: → Compare 325e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Belt Failure (Catastrophic)
problems.symptoms: No symptoms before failure — belt snaps without warning, engine stops, no restart
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000 miles or 4 years — whichever comes first
problems.repairCost: £300–£600 (preventative belt, tensioner and water pump replacement)
problems.replacementCost: £2,500–£5,000 (if belt snaps — full engine rebuild or replacement)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you don't know when the belt was last changed, replace it NOW

problems.recommendation: This is the single most critical maintenance item on the M20 engine. The M20B27 (and ALL M20 engines) are interference engines — if the timing belt breaks, pistons will hit valves. Depending on engine speed, this results in bent valves at minimum, all the way to catastrophic damage requiring a full engine replacement. The belt should be replaced every 60,000 miles or 4 years. If you're buying a 325e with no documented timing belt history, assume it needs doing immediately and use it as a bargaining point. Replace the water pump and tensioner at the same time.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing belt failure guide

problems.title: Vacuum Leaks (Idle Issues)
problems.symptoms: Erratic idle, hunting idle, stalling, rough running, poor fuel economy
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — rubber components age and crack
problems.repairCost: £100–£300 (intake boot, vacuum hoses, valve cover breather hose)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if idle issues develop

problems.recommendation: The M20 engine is very sensitive to vacuum leaks, which cause idle issues. The intake boot and the hose from the valve cover to the intake are prime culprits. Other common failure points include the idle control valve (ICV), ageing main and fuel pump relays, cracked intake boots, and brittle coolant hoses. If you experience rough idle or stalling, start by checking for vacuum leaks — this is often a simple and inexpensive fix.

failureLink.label: Read the full vacuum leak guide → /failures/vacuum-leaks/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Cooling System Ageing
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, temperature gauge fluctuating, steam from engine bay
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — rubber and plastic components age
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice coolant loss or overheating

problems.recommendation: The cooling system on the 325e is ageing — every piece of rubber either has been or will need replacing. Common failures include brittle coolant hoses, the radiator end tanks cracking, and the thermostat sticking. The water pump should be replaced with the timing belt as a matter of course. If you notice the temperature gauge rising, shut the engine down immediately — overheating an M20 can cause head gasket failure or worse.

failureLink.label: Read the full cooling system guide

Interference engine — piston-valve contact requires full rebuild or replacement

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 325e? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing belt replacement within the last 60,000 miles or 4 years — this is non-negotiable
* Full service history with regular oil changes
* No evidence of overheating or coolant loss
* Good body and chassis condition — rust is the real enemy on E30s
avoidIf: * No timing belt history — this is a ticking time bomb
* Overheating history or cooling system issues — the M20 is unforgiving of overheating
* Erratic idle that hasn't been diagnosed — likely vacuum leaks but could be more
* Significant rust — E30s are appreciating classics, but rust repair is expensive

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 325e's M20B27 "Eta" engine is one of BMW's most durable and characterful powerplants — a 2.7-litre straight-six that prioritises low-rev torque and fuel economy over peak horsepower. The "e" stands for efficiency, and the engine delivers: 170 lb-ft of torque at just 3,250 rpm, with an incredibly flat powerband. The key to a long life is simple: change the timing belt on schedule. At 60,000 miles or 4 years, this is non-negotiable. With that done, the M20B27 is capable of 200,000+ miles. Buy on documented history, not on optimism.

cta.label: → Compare 325e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 325e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Timing Belt History — Has it been changed within the last 60,000 miles or 4 years? This is critical
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling, ticking, or rough idle on start-up
* Idle Quality — Does the engine idle smoothly, or does it hunt or stall? (Vacuum leaks)
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; ask about water pump, radiator, thermostat history
* Service History — Including the M20B27 engine and confirmed oil change intervals
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve cover and timing case
* Rust — Check rocker panels, battery box (in trunk), jack storage area, passenger floor boards, bottom of doors
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: * Valvetrain
items.value: SOHC, 2 valves per cylinder (12 valves total), belt-driven

items.label: * Fuel System
